sqlite_rejection.rule: No Handshake runtime, tests, fixtures, mocks, examples, fallbacks, cache, compatibility adapters, temporary harnesses, imports, exports, or product paths may use SQLite.

signals: openDb selects sqlite when provider is not postgres

sqlite_rejection.handshake_translation_note: Use Postgres-first or repository-approved durable storage contracts only; do not port SQLite fallback, fixtures, migration compatibility, adapters, or examples.

key_findings: CKC is useful source evidence for library, character, intake, PoseKit, workflow, ComfyUI, export, settings, and automation surfaces.
CKC backend is concentrated in app/backend/library.js; Handshake should split bounded services rather than copy the monolith.
CKC has stable automation selectors and command surfaces that are valuable for Handshake model-facing usability.
CKC includes both Postgres and SQLite; SQLite is a hard negative example for Handshake and must not appear anywhere.
CKC ComfyUI bridge shows a strong pattern: generation output saved in ComfyUI first, then best-effort product registration through a local intake endpoint.
CKC test behavior areas are reusable as coverage inspiration, but CKC SQLite fixtures and compatibility tests are rejected.
